title: Mathematics
description: RAND has explored, among other math-related topics, mathematics curricula in primary education and the role of mathematics in innovation, e.g., in the evaluation of new medical technologies or the development of methodologies such as game theory.
text:
Assessing the Practical Feasibility of the Clader-Jacobs-Sprouse Quantum Algorithm for Calculating Radar Cross Sections
The authors of this report examine the Clader-Jacobs-Sprouse quantum computing algorithm's potential practical utility for modeling a target's radar cross section.
American Mathematics Educator Survey: 2025 Technical Documentation and Survey Results
This technical report presents information about the sample, survey instruments, and data for the 2025 American Mathematics Educator Study surveys that were administered to principals and teachers in spring 2025 via the American Educator Panels.
Secondary Mathematics Instruction in Classes with Mostly Lower- and Mostly Higher-Achieving Students: Insights from the American Teacher Panel
Assessments indicate that U.S. secondary school students struggle in math. In this report, the authors use a teacher survey and interviews to understand math course placement policies, classroom instruction, and professional learning opportunities.
Harnessing the Benefits of EdTech: What Research Tells Us About Using Digital Technology to Support Pupils
What are the keys to ensuring that educational technology is a genuine driver of student improvement?
How Public School–Based Pre-K Teachers Use and Combine Instructional Materials: Findings from the American Public School Pre-K Teacher Survey
Instructional materials are a crucial aspect of quality early childhood education. This report presents a national picture of how public school–based pre-kindergarten teachers used and combined instructional materials during the 2024–2025 school year.
The RAND Tool for Evaluating Algorithmic Bias: A User Guide
With the RAND Tool for Evaluating Algorithmic Bias, a user can assess and correct performance differences in binary predictive algorithms across groups. This guide explains how to use the tool, even when complete group information is unavailable.
Students Lose Interest in Math: Findings from the American Youth Panel
The authors of this report present new survey data on U.S. middle school and high school students' perceptions of their math class experiences from RAND's newly established American Youth Panel.
Disconnected from Math, Students Call for Real-World Relevance in RAND's First-Ever Youth Survey
New national data show widespread disengagement in math, highlighting a need for more relatable instruction and higher-quality curriculum.
Algorithmic Advancement in Artificial Intelligence: A Survey of Advances with Projections for the Near Future
As artificial intelligence (AI) capabilities grow, so do AI policy concerns. In this report, the authors make projections about the direction, pace, and indicators of algorithmic advances to explore potential AI progress and help inform policymaking.
Prioritizing Curriculum-Based Professional Learning for Mathematics Teachers: District, Principal, and Teacher Perspectives from the American Educator Panels
The authors explore factors in school leaders' selection of math-focused professional learning (PL); teachers' experiences with math-focused PL; perceived usefulness of these activities; and the extent to which math-focused PL is curriculum based.
Methods for Synthetic Data Generation
This book chapter describes the statistical foundation for generating synthetic data in order to facilitate data sharing while protecting privacy.
